zh.lifeSummary: 當Margrett Ann Parkham於1620年出生在英國維爾爾時，她的父親Mathew James Parham22歲，母親Frances Holmes14歲。她於1639年與William Isaac Smith在英國英格蘭維爾爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1708年1月20日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 約克 可隆尼素福爾可斯米托享年88歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margrett Ann Parkham于1620年出生在英国維爾爾时，她的父亲Mathew James Parham22岁，母亲Frances Holmes14岁。她于1639年与William Isaac Smith在英国英格兰維爾爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1708年1月20日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 約克 可隆尼素福爾可斯米托享年88岁。

nameMeanings.name: Parkham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name, perhaps from Parnham in Beaminster (Dorset), which is recorded as Perham in 1228 and Parnham in 1431. The placename appears to derive from Old English peru ‘pear’ + hām ‘village, homestead’ or hamm ‘water meadow’. Alternatively, perhaps from a lost or unidentified place so named.

nameMeanings.name: Margrett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: relationship name from the Middle English female personal name Margaret. Compare Margary and Margot . Note also Margetson .

variants.name: Margretts
meanings: English: variant of Margrett with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margary
meanings: The usual medieval vernacular form of   Margaret   (now also commonly spelled   Marjorie  ). This form of the name is preserved in the nursery rhyme ‘See-saw, Margery Daw’.

variants.name: Margret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margetson
meanings: English: relationship name from the Middle English female personal name Margaret ( see Margrett ) + the patronymic marker -son.

variants.name: Margot
meanings: pet form of   Marguerite  , now used as an independent given name. In England it is still usually pronounced in the French way, but in Eastern Europe the final consonant is sounded, and this has had some influence in America.

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.
After the English took over in the 1660s, the colony was renamed New York, after the Duke of York

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Margrett Ann Parkham was born in 1620, in Wiltshire, England, her father, Mathew James Parham, was 22 and her mother, Frances Holmes, was 14. She married William Isaac Smith in 1639, in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 20 January 1708, in Smithtown, Suffolk, New York Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 88.

ko.lifeSummary: Margrett Ann Parkham(이)가 1620년 영국 윌츠히르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Mathew James Parham(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Frances Holmes(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 1639년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에서 William Isaac Smith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1708년 1월 20일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 요르크 콜론이 수폴크 스미스타운에서 88세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margrett Ann Parkham родилась в 1620 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия, её отцу по имени Mathew James Parham было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Frances Holmes, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Isaac Smith в 1639 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 20 января 1708 г. в местности смиттоун, суффолк, ню ерк колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 88 лет.

Se casó con William Isaac Smith en 1639, en Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 20 de enero de 1708, en Smithtown, Suffolk, New York Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 88 años.
